name: archive-retention
question: Which archived PODS sessions exist, and which are old enough to sweep under the retention threshold?

schema.dirs: array of {sessionId, createdAt, ageDays, sizeBytes, kept, sweepable, reason}

_dirEntry_schema.sessionId: string (basename of the archived dir, e.g. 'collab-20260225-004014')
_dirEntry_schema.createdAt: string|null (ISO-8601 UTC parsed from sessionId; null if unparsable)
_dirEntry_schema.ageDays: integer|null (days between createdAt and now; null if createdAt unparsable)
_dirEntry_schema.sizeBytes: integer (recursive byte count of the dir)
_dirEntry_schema.sweepable: boolean (true if --gc would sweep this dir)

consultation.typical_sessions_using: every (cheap mode in session-start hook); on-demand for full sweep

fallback: Read .claude/collaboration/archived/ directly; for each collab-* subdir, parse sessionId timestamp suffix and compare against (now - threshold). Skip dirs containing a KEEP file. Before any delete, append a JSON line summarizing the dir to .claude/collaboration/archived/_summary.jsonl.

gcMode.summary: Archive retention sweep (RETENTION-01..06). Sweeps .claude/collaboration/archived/collab-*/ for dirs where ageDays > threshold (default 90) AND no KEEP file is present. Before each delete, appends a JSON entry to _summary.jsonl preserving sessionId, createdAt, sweptAt, sizeBytes, workerCount, taskCount, criticVerdict, hasOverrideVeto, guideHeadline (audit trail preserved).

cheap.behavior: Session-start path. If the per-dir scan + summary write + delete loop exceeds 100ms wall-time, the sweep aborts cleanly and defers to the next session-start (graceful absence). Output is empty on success; warning on stderr on failure.
cheap.exit_codes: Always 0 (graceful). Internal failures are logged to stderr and never block the session-start briefing.

on_demand.behavior: Deeper scan with no time budget. Reports a single-line JSON summary on stdout: {swept, before, after, threshold, thresholdSource, summarized, sweptIds}.

summary_schema.summarized: integer (entries appended to _summary.jsonl; equals swept on clean runs)
summary_schema.sweptIds: string (comma-separated sessionIds deleted; omitted if empty)

gcMode.criteria: Subdir basename matches /^collab-\d{8}-\d{6}$/ AND no KEEP file at <dir>/KEEP AND createdAt parsed from basename is older than (now - threshold). Default threshold: 90 days. Unparsable-age dirs are NEVER swept (failure-open).
gcMode.action: 1) Read metadata (sizeBytes, workerCount, taskCount, criticVerdict, hasOverrideVeto, guideHeadline). 2) Append JSON line to .claude/collaboration/archived/_summary.jsonl (atomic append). 3) Verify summary write succeeded (read-back last line). 4) rm -rf the dir. Summary write must succeed before delete; on summary write failure, the dir is NOT deleted.
gcMode.atomic_semantics: Per-dir delete sequence is sequential and uses POSIX-atomic append for _summary.jsonl (each entry < PIPE_BUF / 4KB, atomic across concurrent appenders). No directory-level lock is acquired -- concurrent sweeps from different sessions are race-tolerant: each sweep reads dirs that exist at its scan time; if two sweeps target the same dir, the second one's rm -rf becomes a no-op (idempotent). _summary.jsonl may receive duplicate entries in the rare double-sweep case, which is acceptable (audit trail favors over-recording).
gcMode.threshold_config: Configurable via .claude/config.json key 'archiveRetention.threshold' (numeric days OR ISO-8601 duration string PnD). Falls back to default 90 days if config absent or malformed.

provenance.note: Caps unbounded growth of .claude/collaboration/archived/ from converged PODS sessions. Mirrors CSI-05's --gc / --gc-cheap pattern but operates on filesystem dirs instead of registry entries. KEEP-file pin allows operators to mark sessions for permanent retention (e.g., reference forensic artifacts). Pre-delete _summary.jsonl preserves a one-line audit record per swept session. 90-day default threshold covers Phase 2 dogfooding (2026-05-18) and quarterly retrospectives; configurable via .claude/config.json.
